mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals expanding economic output and rising corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month as investors discount improved earnings.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year correlation between INDPRO 3-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or below +0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% indicates labor market slack and weakening consumer demand, leading to downward revisions in earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In at least 60% of triggered instances (UNRATE > 4.0%), S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive, or the mean return difference from untriggered periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds the year-over-year change in CPI, real interest rates become restrictive. This joint condition of high nominal rates and elevated inflation tightens financial conditions and depresses equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In episodes where FEDFUNDS exceeds CPI YoY change, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 40% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth in the early phase of the economic cycle boosts corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon. The same growth in a late-cycle regime would have opposite effects due to overheating risks.
failure_condition: In early-cycle quarters defined by NBER, the correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and S&P 500 60-day forward returns is less than +0.15 or the mean return is not significantly positive (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects broad hiring strength and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Spearman rank correlation between PAYEMS 1-month change and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is negative or below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.2 million annualized units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, triggering immediate negative reassessments of economic growth that weigh on S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: In at least half of the triggered weeks (HOUST < 1.2M), S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive, or the mean return is not significantly negative (one-tailed p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production grows faster than retail sales (INDPRO 3-month change minus RSAFS 3-month change exceeds 2%), it indicates inventory overhang relative to demand. Firms will cut production or discount, compressing margins and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth exceeds RSAFS 3-month growth by more than 2 percentage points
failure_condition: In episodes where the divergence exceeds 2%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate reaches or exceeds 5% per annum, policy is explicitly restrictive and tightens financial conditions, increasing the discount rate on equities and reducing future cash flow expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trading days when FEDFUNDS >= 5%, S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive, or the Sharpe ratio over those periods is greater than 0.3.
